summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the repository root whose targets/sources fields use GYP command-expansion syntax (<!(...)) at line 6. npm implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present, even without any declared install/postinstall script, and node-gyp evaluates <!(...) by spawning a shell during the configure step. This causes attacker-controlled commands embedded in binding.gyp to execute automatically on `npm install`, functionally equivalent to a postinstall lifecycle hook. The package is published as a Backstage backend plugin (TypeScript) for which a native addon and a binding.gyp have no legitimate purpose; no.c/.cc/.cpp/.h sources are part of this plugin's normal build. the analysis engaged but its output was withheld by the provider safety filter, which is consistent with malicious content in the traced files.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
